content.text:
<user_query>
Read and return the relevant sections from these architecture docs in the Roamera V2 project. I need the auth-related sections:

1. /Users/aryanchaudhary/Downloads/fullstack-main/docs/architecture/06-system-architecture.md ‚Äî Read the FULL file but especially focus on sections about:
   - Auth (JWT, bcrypt, sessions, refresh tokens, ws_token)
   - Database schema (section 4.2) ‚Äî specifically the users, sessions, follows tables
   - File storage (section 6)
   - Rate limiting
   - Environment variables / config

2. /Users/aryanchaudhary/Downloads/fullstack-main/docs/architecture/07-api-surface.md ‚Äî Read the FULL file but especially focus on:
   - All /auth/* endpoints (register, login, OTP, refresh, logout, password reset, ws-token, me)
   - All /users/* endpoints (profile, update, avatar, follow, search, settings)
   - Request/response schemas for each endpoint
   - Error codes

Return ALL relevant content in full detail.
</user_query>

Reading both architecture documents and extracting auth-related sections.

[REDACTED]

Here is what those two architecture docs actually contain. **`07-api-surface.md` does not define per-field JSON bodies or a REST error-code catalog**; it lists Zod schema names and notes global rules. The only structured ‚Äúerror‚Äù payload called out there is for **WebSocket** messages.

---

## `/Users/aryanchaudhary/Downloads/fullstack-main/docs/architecture/06-system-architecture.md`

### Service responsibility ‚Äî `auth`

From ¬ß3.1:

| Module | Responsibility |
|--------|-----------------|
| `auth` | Register, login, JWT issue/refresh, email verify, OTP, password reset, account delete, session list, avatar upload |

### Web (`apps/web`) ‚Äî middleware (JWT)

From ¬ß3.3:

- **Middleware:** auth guard via JWT cookie check, locale detection

### ¬ß4.2 Schema ‚Äî `users`, `sessions`, `follows` (and adjacent identity tables)

From the Identity / Social blocks:

```
Identity
  users                  id, username, email, password_hash, role, avatar_key,
                         home_city, bio, budget_band, interests[], email_verified,
                         created_at, updated_at
  sessions               id, user_id, token_hash, expires_at, created_at
  otp_tokens             id, user_id, email, code_hash, expires_at, used_at
  password_reset_tokens  id, user_id, token_hash, expires_at, used_at
  invite_tokens          id, created_by, token, max_uses, uses, expires_at
  user_settings          user_id, key, value (per-user key/value store)
  audit_log              id, user_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, details, ip, created_at

Social
  ...
  follows                follower_id, following_id, created_at
  ...
```

### ¬ß5.1 WebSocket ‚Äî `ws_token` lifecycle

The doc specifies **POST** `/api/v1/auth/ws-token` with JWT, response `{ token: "short-lived ws_token (10min TTL)" }`, then connect with `wss://.../ws?token=<ws_token>`, validate, `connected` with `user_id`, subscribe flow, ping/pong, reconnect with fresh token.

(Sequence diagram and room/event tables continue in the same section.)

### ¬ß6 File / object storage

```
STORAGE_DRIVER=local   ‚Üí multer writes to data/uploads/{yyyy}/{mm}/{sha256.ext}
STORAGE_DRIVER=r2      ‚Üí @aws-sdk/client-s3 pointing to R2 bucket (S3-compatible)
```

**Production (R2):**

- Upload: Node API generates presigned PUT URL ‚Üí client uploads directly to R2 ‚Üí client confirms key to API  
- Download public: direct R2 public URL (post photos, destination covers)  
- Download gated: time-limited presigned GET URL (trip files, invoices)  
- Thumbnails: `sharp` on upload ‚Üí `{key}_thumb.webp`  
- Content-addressed: `sha256(content).ext`; dedup via `uploads.ref_count`

### ¬ß9 Auth flows (JWT, bcrypt, sessions, refresh, OTP)

**9.1 Email + password**

- `POST /auth/register` ‚Üí bcrypt hash, user with `email_verified=false`, verification email (Resend), `201 { message: "verify your email" }`  
- `GET /auth/verify-email?token=` ‚Üí set `email_verified=true`, `200 { message: "verified" }`  
- `POST /auth/login` ‚Üí bcrypt compare, **create session**, sign **JWT (15 min) + refresh (7 d)**, `200 { accessToken, refreshToken, user }`

**9.2 Email OTP**

- `POST /auth/otp/send` ‚Üí 6-digit code, hash + store **10 min TTL**, Resend, `200 { message: "code sent" }`  
- `POST /auth/otp/verify` ‚Üí compare hash, mark used, **issue JWT + refresh**, `200 { accessToken, refreshToken, user }`

**9.3 Token refresh**

```
POST /auth/refresh { refreshToken }
‚Üí Rotate refresh token (old invalidated), issue new access + refresh pair
```

### ¬ß11.1 Required environment variables (API / AI / Web)

**`apps/api`**

```
DATABASE_URL=libsql://...            # Turso remote (prod) or file:data/app.db (dev)
DATABASE_AUTH_TOKEN=...              # Turso auth token (prod only)
JWT_SECRET=...
JWT_REFRESH_SECRET=...
AI_SERVICE_URL=http://ai-service:8000
AI_SERVICE_SECRET=...                # HMAC signing secret
STORAGE_DRIVER=r2                    # local | r2
R2_ACCOUNT_ID=...
R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID=...
R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=...
R2_BUCKET_NAME=...
RESEND_API_KEY=...
SENTRY_DSN=...
CORS_ORIGINS=https://roamera.in,https://www.roamera.in
PORT=3000
NODE_ENV=production
AMADEUS_CLIENT_ID=...
AMADEUS_CLIENT_SECRET=...
PEXELS_API_KEY=...
FOURSQUARE_API_KEY=...               # optional
```

**`apps/ai-service`** ‚Äî `AI_PROVIDER`, `AI_FALLBACK_PROVIDER`, provider API keys, `AI_SERVICE_SECRET`, `PORT`.

**`apps/web`** ‚Äî `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL`, PostHog/Sentry public keys.

### ¬ß14 Security ‚Äî rate limiting (and related auth notes)

| Concern | Mechanism |
|---------|-----------|
| Rate limiting | `express-rate-limit` **per IP + per user** (**stricter on auth routes**) |
| CSRF | API uses Bearer tokens; WS uses `ws_token` |
| (also: CORS whitelist, helmet, Zod, Drizzle, XSS, HMAC S2S, presigned assets, idempotency, audit log) |

### `packages/types` pointer (auth Zod names)

From ¬ß8:

- `auth.ts` ‚Äî `UserSchema`, `LoginSchema`, `RegisterSchema`, `OtpSchema`

---

## `/Users/aryanchaudhary/Downloads/fullstack-main/docs/architecture/07-api-surface.md`

### Global rules (top of file)

- Base path: **`/api/v1/`**  
- Auth: **`Authorization: Bearer <accessToken>`** unless `[public]`  
- JSON bodies; uploads `multipart/form-data`  
- Mutations: **`Idempotency-Key: <uuid>`**  
- Schema names refer to **`packages/types/src/schemas/`**

### ¬ß1 Auth ‚Äî `/api/v1/auth`

| Method | Path | Auth | Description | Zod Schema |
|--------|------|------|-------------|------------|
| `POST` | `/register` | [public] | Create account with email + password | `RegisterSchema` |
| `POST` | `/login` | [public] | Email + password login | `LoginSchema` |
| `POST` | `/otp/send` | [public] | Send 6-digit OTP to email | `OtpSendSchema` |
| `POST` | `/otp/verify` | [public] | Verify OTP, issue JWT | `OtpVerifySchema` |
| `GET` | `/verify-email` | [public] | `?token=` ‚Äî verify email address | ‚Äî |
| `POST` | `/refresh` | [public] | Rotate refresh token, issue new pair | `RefreshSchema` |
| `POST` | `/logout` | auth | Invalidate session | ‚Äî |
| `POST` | `/password/reset-request` | [public] | Send password reset email | `PasswordResetRequestSchema` |
| `POST` | `/password/reset` | [public] | Consume token, set new password | `PasswordResetSchema` |
| `POST` | `/password/change` | auth | Change password (requires current) | `PasswordChangeSchema` |
| `GET` | `/me` | auth | Current user profile | ‚Äî |
| `GET` | `/ws-token` | auth | Issue ephemeral WebSocket token **(10 min TTL)** | ‚Äî |

V1 ‚Üí V2 mapping table is also in this section.

### ¬ß2 Users ‚Äî `/api/v1/users`

| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
|--------|------|------|-------------|
| `GET` | `/:username` | auth | Public profile by username |
| `PATCH` | `/me` | auth | Update bio, home city, avatar, interests, budget band |
| `POST` | `/me/avatar` | auth | Upload avatar (multipart; ‚Üí R2) |
| `DELETE` | `/me` | auth | Delete account (soft-delete, 30d grace) |
| `GET` | `/search` | auth | `?q=` user search |
| `GET` | `/:userId/followers` | auth | Follower list (paginated) |
| `GET` | `/:userId/following` | auth | Following list (paginated) |
| `POST` | `/:userId/follow` | auth | Follow a user |
| `DELETE` | `/:userId/follow` | auth | Unfollow a user |
| `GET` | `/me/settings` | auth | Key/value user settings |
| `PUT` | `/me/settings` | auth | Bulk upsert settings |

### Request/response schemas (per endpoint)

In **this** doc, they are **not spelled out field-by-field**. Each body is tied to a **Zod schema name** where listed (`RegisterSchema`, `LoginSchema`, etc.). Shapes like `{ accessToken, refreshToken, user }` for login appear in **¬ß9 of `06-system-architecture.md`**, not in duplicated form in ¬ß1 of `07-api-surface.md` for every route.

### Error codes

**REST:** `07-api-surface.md` does **not** enumerate HTTP status codes or `{ code, message }` bodies for `/auth/*` or `/users/*`.

**WebSocket (¬ß21):** server ‚Üí client event:

| Type | Payload | Trigger |
|------|---------|---------|
| `error` | `{ code, message }` | Auth fail, room access denied |

---

**Summary:** `06-system-architecture.md` defines bcrypt/JWT/session/refresh/OTP/`ws_token` behavior, the `users` / `sessions` / `follows` schema lines, R2 vs local storage, stricter rate limits on auth routes, and JWT-related env vars. `07-api-surface.md` catalogs `/api/v1/auth/*` and `/api/v1/users/*` with Zod names and auth requirements but does not document full JSON schemas or REST error codes beyond the general WS `error` event. For concrete Zod fields, the next place to look is `packages/types/src/schemas/auth.ts` (and any `users` schema file) ‚Äî not these markdown files.

[REDACTED]
